questions.q: The equilibrium level of employment is determined at the point where the aggregate demand curve intersects the
Which of the following is a short-run equilibrium situation?
questions.answer: When the aggregate demand curve intersects the aggregate supply curve

questions.q: Explain the concept of full employment equilibrium in the context of the AD-AS model.
questions.answer: Full employment equilibrium occurs when the aggregate demand curve intersects the short-run aggregate supply curve at a point where the economy is operating at its full employment level.

questions.q: What is the difference between the short-run aggregate supply curve and the long-run aggregate supply curve?
questions.answer: The short-run aggregate supply curve is a downward-sloping curve, while the long-run aggregate supply curve is a horizontal line.

questions.q: Suppose the aggregate demand curve shifts to the right due to an increase in government expenditure. What will happen to the equilibrium level of employment?
questions.answer: The equilibrium level of employment will increase.

questions.q: What is the concept of the multiplier effect in the context of the AD-AS model?
questions.answer: The multiplier effect is the change in equilibrium output resulting from a change in autonomous expenditure.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of the long-run aggregate supply curve?
Explain the concept of the short-run aggregate supply curve.
questions.answer: The short-run aggregate supply curve is a downward-sloping curve that represents the relationship between the price level and the level of output in the short run.

questions.q: What is the difference between the AD-AS model and the Keynesian cross diagram?
questions.answer: The AD-AS model represents the interactions between the aggregate demand and aggregate supply curves, while the Keynesian cross diagram represents the intersections of the aggregate demand curve and the aggregate supply curve.

questions.q: Suppose the aggregate supply curve shifts to the left due to a decrease in productivity. What will happen to the equilibrium price level and the equilibrium level of employment?
questions.answer: The equilibrium price level will increase, and the equilibrium level of employment will decrease.
